behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry-state handoff across modules (attempt counter + backoff policy + scheduler), not just pure math. A unit test of `nextDelay(attempt)` can pass while integration fails if attempt state resets between retries. Rejected System: behavior text only requires in-process retry timing; fake clock + stub transport reproduces failure without real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML decision or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry controller, backoff policy, and timer abstraction with a stub API that fails repeatedly. Assert observed scheduled delays are 100, 200, 400... capped at configured max, using fake timers to advance time and inspect actual retry scheduling.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is at a module boundary: HTTP response classification (e.g., 429/headers) must feed delay computation logic. Unit-testing classifier alone misses wiring bugs (wrong header read, wrong precedence). Rejected System: a stubbed response object can reproduce classification and wait computation exactly; no real network needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub transport responses with combinations of rate-limit signals (status/header/body metadata). Verify retry module classifies only rate-limit cases and computes the expected delay input to scheduler for each case.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Requires orchestration across wait scheduler, request replay, and result propagation to caller. Unit tests of resend function alone miss failures like mutated request payload or dropped promise resolution path. Rejected System: behavior can be validated with in-process stub transport plus fake timer; real HTTP stack not required by text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic non-LLM flow.
behaviors.test_description: Set first stub response to rate-limit, second to success. Invoke `call()` once, advance fake time to the computed wait, assert transport received the same original request twice and caller receives final success value without retry-specific leakage.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Typed error emission depends on retry-loop termination + error mapping path together. A unit test of error class construction is insufficient if loop exits through a different branch. Rejected System: repeated stubbed failures are enough to drive max-retry branch; no real infrastructure required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries (e.g., 3), stub API to always return rate-limit, run `call()`, and assert rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected metadata (attempt count/quota context) and not a generic transport error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans multiple local modules (retry driver + backoff + quota tracker), so unit is too low. Rejected System: despite timing concerns, infinite-loop risk here is logic/state progression that fake timers and stub transport can expose without real OS/network. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: With always-rate-limited stub responses and bounded max retries, run `call()` under fake timers and assert: exact number of attempts, quota tracker decrements/locks as expected, and promise settles with terminal error within bounded simulated time (no additional scheduled retries).

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears only when concurrent callers interact through shared state across modules (public client API + quota manager + dispatcher). Unit tests on quota object alone can miss per-client-instance wiring mistakes. Rejected System: behavior text says "within the same process" and does not require real network/OS primitives; in-process concurrency test is sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI components.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple concurrent `call()` invocations against one library process with controlled stub responses. Assert all calls consume the same quota counter/bucket instance and exhaustion seen by one caller is reflected consistently across others.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: FIFO correctness depends on queue + scheduler + dispatch integration under async contention; single-function unit tests can miss race/order bugs at handoff boundaries. Rejected System: deterministic in-process queueing with controlled timers reproduces ordering failures; no real HTTP/subprocess needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM steps.
behaviors.test_description: Submit several calls quickly while dispatch is blocked by rate-limit wait. Release capacity and advance fake time; assert transport dispatch order matches submission order and completion order reflects FIFO where responses are uniform.
